Ad Text:
Technical Writer - Defence (Baseline Cleared)

Immediate Start | Pathway into Procurement Analyst

Our client is a high-performing boutique consulting firm supporting major programs within the Australian defence industry. They are seeking an experienced Technical Writer to join a project team delivering SOPs, training content, guidance material and process documentation in a complex Federal Government / Defence environment.

This is a hands-on role for someone who can work with subject matter experts, analyse business processes and system outputs, and turn complex information into clear, practical documentation that supports new ways of working.

For the right person, this role offers a genuine pathway into a Procurement Analyst position following the initial engagement period.

About the role

You will work closely with SMEs and project stakeholders to develop high-quality documentation, training material and supporting project artefacts within a complex enterprise environment.

This role will suit someone who can work autonomously, communicate well with a broad stakeholder group, and produce polished, structured outputs in a fast-paced project setting.

What you’ll be doing

Develop and finalise standard operating procedures, guidance documents, user guides and process documentation

Create training materials, eLearning content, presentations and workshop packs for a range of user groups

Liaise with SMEs to gather, validate and structure information for review, approval and publishing

Review business processes, workflows and system outputs to identify documentation gaps, inefficiencies and training needs

Support project delivery through governance, change and stakeholder reporting artefacts

Contribute to a collaborative, high-performing project team

What we’re looking for
Essential

Current AGSVA Baseline Security Clearance

Ability to start immediately

Minimum 5 years’ experience in Technical Writing, business/process analysis, or closely related documentation-focused roles

Strong ability to translate complex information into clear, accurate content for both technical and non-technical audiences

Proven ability to work independently, manage competing priorities and engage effectively with stakeholders

Strong written communication skills, attention to detail and document quality control capability

Exposure to procurement functions, processes, or support activities within the defence sector.

Highly regarded

Previous experience in a defence.

Exposure to ERP, business process documentation, SOP development or systems-based project work.

Background in procurement, commercial analysis, supply chain, logistics or analyst-style roles.

Interest in transitioning into a Procurement Analyst pathway over time.

Why apply

Immediate-start opportunity in a Defence environment

Meaningful work across documentation, training, stakeholder engagement and process analysis

A genuine opportunity to transition into a Procurement Analyst role for a strong performer

A role suited to someone who wants more than pure document writing and is looking to broaden their career over time

Apply now

If you hold a current Baseline clearance, are available to start immediately, and are looking for a Technical Writer role with genuine progression into analysis and procurement, we’d like to hear from you.

Insighture is a leading technology consultancy that drives digital transformation for businesses worldwide. With a team of over 85 expert consultants, the company delivers tailored, high-impact strategies and solutions, enabling scalable product engineering. As an AWS partner, Insighture excels in co-integrated cloud services. It has collaborated with more than 50 clients globally, guiding them through cloud adoption, DevOps transformation, enterprise modernisation, and more.

The team’s expertise spans Cloud-Native Development, Solutions Architecture, UI/UX, Quality Engineering, Data Engineering, AI/ML, and DevSecOps. These capabilities empower businesses to achieve impactful and innovative outcomes.

In 2024, Insighture achieved ISO certification and was recognised as a Great Place to Work, earning three prestigious awards: Best Workplace in Sri Lanka, Best Workplace for Technology, and Best Workplace for Young People. Insighture's technology and expertise are embedded in the work of internationally recognised care providers, global freight operations, child protection systems, and health tech platforms across Australia, the UK, and Singapore.

We are seeking an experienced Pega LSA / Architect with deep expertise in enterprise-grade Pega implementations to lead architecture, design, and delivery of scalable and high-performing solutions. This role requires strong leadership, hands-on development capability, and the ability to guide teams while ensuring adherence to Pega best practices and guardrails.




Responsibilities

Design and own end-to-end Pega architecture, including High-Level Design (HLD) and Low-Level Design (LLD)

Lead and execute Pega implementations, ensuring scalability, performance, and adherence to best practices

Break down user stories into actionable technical tasks and guide the team on execution

Provide hands-on support in development, troubleshooting, and issue resolution

Ensure compliance with Pega guardrails, standards, and implementation methodologies

Manage and govern documentation across Confluence/SharePoint

Drive daily status reporting and stakeholder communication with leadership and clients

Prioritize tasks effectively based on urgency and business needs

Manage deliverables and ensure continuity of work in the absence of team members

Collaborate with CoE teams and contribute to architecture governance

Support estimation activities for RFPs and RFIs

Mentor junior team members and provide technical guidance to team leads

Design and implement workflows, case management solutions, and integrations

Identify opportunities for optimization, automation, and improved delivery




Requirements

Pega LSA (Lead System Architect) certified

CLSA certified on Pega 8.x (Infinity experience preferred)

10+ years of overall Pega experience in enterprise environments

Strong experience in Pega installation, including Version 24.2

Hands-on experience in developing workflows, user interfaces, activities, utility, and decision rules

Strong understanding of ECS, Situational Layer Cake, and Case Management

Excellent knowledge of SDLC, Agile, and Pega implementation methodologies

Experience with external integrations including traditional and DX APIs

Strong experience with PRPC reporting tools and performance tuning

Expertise in debugging tools such as Clipboard, Tracer, Rules Inspector, PDC, and AES

Experience with Correspondence Rules, Validation Rules, and Integration Rules

Hands-on experience with Agents, Job Schedulers, Channels, and Skin rules

Strong SQL knowledge (DML, DQL; DDL preferred)

Experience with Constellation framework

Exposure to Kafka, Kubernetes, and cloud technologies

Familiarity with Blueprint, Gen-AI, and Knowledge Buddy integrations

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills

Ability to lead teams while remaining hands-on in delivery
